content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer NNN.

Construct a permutation PPP of length NNN such that


For all iii (1‚Č§i‚Č§N‚ąí1)(1 \leq i \leq N-1)(1‚Č§i‚Č§N‚ąí1), iii divides abs(Pi+1‚ąíPi)abs(P_{i+1}-P_i)abs(Pi+1‚Äč‚ąíPi‚Äč).
Recall that a permutation of length NNN is an array where every integer from 111 to NNN occurs exactly once.

It can be proven that for the given constraints at least one such PPP always exists.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follow.
The only line of each test case contains an integer NNN - the length of the array to be constructed.


Output Format
For each test case, output a single line containing NNN space-separated integers P1,P2,‚Ä¶,PNP_1, P_2, \dots, P_NP1‚Äč,P2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,PN‚Äč, denoting the elements of the array PPP.


If there exist multiple such arrays, print any.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105.

For i=1i=1i=1: abs(A2‚ąíA1)=abs(2‚ąí1)=1abs(A_2-A_1)=abs(2-1)=1abs(A2‚Äč‚ąíA1‚Äč)=abs(2‚ąí1)=1 is divisible by 111.

Test case 2: A possible array satisfying all the conditions is [2,1,3][2, 1, 3][2,1,3]:

For i=1i=1i=1: abs(A2‚ąíA1)=abs(1‚ąí2)=1abs(A_2-A_1)=abs(1-2)=1abs(A2‚Äč‚ąíA1‚Äč)=abs(1‚ąí2)=1 is divisible by 111.
For i=2i=2i=2: abs(A3‚ąíA2)=abs(3‚ąí1)=2abs(A_3-A_2)=abs(3-1)=2abs(A3‚Äč‚ąíA2‚Äč)=abs(3‚ąí1)=2 is divisible by 222.
